archetypes.title: reed apothecary
descriptions: Bundles at the apothecary's belt distinguish fever-reed, roof-reed, and the reed burned when soldiers ask questions.
Wet green perfume follows the apothecary, with grave salt underneath.
wants: Keep Hollowmere's quiet shelters supplied without teaching the occupiers which reeds hide doors.
Trade enough medicine to replace what the last patrol tipped into the water.

services.name: reed remedy
services.description: A private preparation for pain, fever, and the memory of pursuit.

archetypes.title: ferry oathkeeper
descriptions: Crossing-notches run up both forearms, interrupted where one name was carefully cut away.
The oathkeeper smells of willow tar and water that has traveled farther than maps.
wants: Guide dangerous travelers by crossings that cost the village least.
Learn who has been taking the unmarked ferry after moonset.

services.name: quiet crossing
services.description: The oathkeeper marks a reed-hidden route that avoids the loud bridges.

archetypes.title: eel-cutter
descriptions: Silver scales freckle an oilskin apron; none belong to an ordinary eel.
A crescent knife moves between practical work and household omen.
wants: Find what has been teaching the trap-eels to return imperial tags instead of fish.
Get one dry night to mend the household roof before the next occupation search.

archetypes.title: shelter-aunt
descriptions: The aunt carries three door keys and denies that any fit a door.
Red thread at the cuff means hospitality; the knot in it means not yet.
wants: Decide whether this fugitive is worth risking three hidden beds and a nephew in custody.
Get a warning to the next household before the inspection punt arrives.

services.name: a hidden bed
services.description: The aunt risks one of three hidden beds so you can close your eyes and heal.

archetypes.title: Free Folk lookout
descriptions: They watch the causeway from a punt that never quite finishes its errand.
A reed whistle and a memorised list of names they will not write down.
wants: Judge whether this bright-handed fugitive is a weapon the cell can trust or a fire that will take the whole marsh.
Learn the reaping schedule before the sweep reaches Ferryman's Knot.

services.name: a watcher's word
services.description: The lookout marks which lanes the inspection punts are watching tonight.
services.targetHint: a lane the Empire is not watching

archetypes.title: Free Folk runner
descriptions: Young, fast, and certain the cell is too careful by half.
Mud to the knees from carrying warnings the long way round the checkpoints.
wants: Use the sorcerer NOW to break the reaping open, whatever the older heads say.
Get a warning to the next household before the inspection punt arrives.

archetypes.title: Free Folk shelterwright
descriptions: They know which drowned houses still have a dry floor and a hidden hearth.
A ring of odd keys and a memory for who owes the cell what.
wants: Turn a warned village into a place the cell can rest, cache, and come back to.
Keep the safe-house secret from the one runner who cannot hold their tongue.

services.name: shelter and rest
services.description: A dry hidden floor where the sorcerer can recover out of the Empire's sight.

services.name: a cache drop
services.description: The shelterwright remembers a hidden cache for you, and where the cell left supplies.

archetypes.title: Kindled sympathizer
descriptions: They speak of answering the Shadow Purge with a purge of the Empire's own.
The older Free Folk sit a little apart from them, and do not quite disagree out loud.
wants: Convince the cell that terror and the old possession-craft are the only language the Empire hears.
Recruit the sorcerer to the Kindled way before the cautious heads talk them out of it.

archetypes.title: reconciled householder
descriptions: They pay the tally on time and sleep, and want their neighbours to do the same.
A census token worn openly, and a door that stays shut after moonset.
wants: Keep the roads open and the family off the reaping list by giving the Empire no reason to look here.
Refuse the fugitive politely, because the last house that helped is two names shorter.

archetypes.title: marsh menagerie-healer
descriptions: Three incompatible kinds of fur cling to an apron pocket full of warm river stones.
A reed whistle at their throat speaks more dialects than the healer admits to knowing.
wants: Calm the imported glass-antler kit before an inspector classifies its panic as wild magic.
Convince one wealthy household to pay for the mud-swallow's medicine instead of buying a prettier animal.

services.name: creature calming
services.description: The healer teaches a frightened creature your smell and leaves it a calm memory of your hands.
services.targetHint: a nearby frightened or escaped creature
